metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete 'Verify:' clause to AC3 — e.g. a grep that confirms any remaining bot-identity strings appear ONLY in explicitly excluded paths (`git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot' -- | grep -vE '^(infra/|\.github/workflows/|tests/|docs/experiments/)' | grep -c .` exits non-zero / returns 0 matches).
evidence: AC3 text: 'Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it (e.g. infra/, .github/workflows/, CLAUDE.local.md) and is not presented as general contributor guidance.'
No 'Verify:' clause appears after AC3, unlike AC1, AC2, and AC5 which each have a 'Verify:' clause with a concrete command.
findings.finding: AC3 ('Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it') has no concrete proving command; the criterion relies entirely on manual inspection of file placement/context, with no deterministic check that would produce evidence on success.
scenarios: An executor completes all grep-verified ACs but leaves a bot-identity reference in a narrowly-named file that is technically outside infra/ but still general-audience (e.g. docs/onboarding.md); AC3 passes silently because there is no command to run.
A reviewer cannot distinguish 'explicitly scoped' from 'incidentally present' without a deterministic test.
findings.suggested_fix: Append to AC3: "Verify: `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart'` returns results ONLY in paths under `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/`, or `docs/experiments/`; any match outside those prefixes fails the check."

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a 'Verify:' clause to AC4 that grep-checks CONTRIBUTING.md and `.agents/rules/rebar.md` (in addition to AGENTS.md) for the presence of 'git config user.name' and 'git config user.email', or expand the AC1 command to cover all three files.
evidence: AC4 text: 'all three name the committer's own configured git identity (git config user.name/user.email) as the sign-off source'
AC1 Verify clause: `grep -q 'git config user.name' AGENTS.md && grep -q 'git config user.email' AGENTS.md` — covers AGENTS.md only.

AC4 has no 'Verify:' clause at all; its second sub-condition ('none of them names a specific third party') is covered by AC2's grep, but the first sub-condition ('all three name...') is not covered for CONTRIBUTING.md or .agents/rules/rebar.md.
findings.finding: AC4's positive assertion — that CONTRIBUTING.md and .agents/rules/rebar.md each name 'the committer's own configured git identity (git config user.name/user.email)' — has no proving command; the only command in AC1 checks AGENTS.md, leaving the other two files unverified.
scenarios: An executor updates AGENTS.md (satisfying AC1) but leaves CONTRIBUTING.md's DCO section unchanged; AC1 passes, AC2 passes, and AC4 has no command to catch the gap.
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC1's verify command or add to AC4: `grep -q 'git config user.name' CONTRIBUTING.md && grep -q 'git config user.email' CONTRIBUTING.md && grep -q 'git config user.name' .agents/rules/rebar.md && grep -q 'git config user.email' .agents/rules/rebar.md`.

evidence: 4 criterion line(s) bundle multiple deliverables with 'and' (split so each is independently verifiable).

verify command `grep -q 'git config user.name' AGENTS.md && grep -q 'git config user.email' AGENTS.md`.` greps the unanchored word 'git' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_git'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"git":')

verify command `grep -q 'git config user.name' AGENTS.md && grep -q 'git config user.email' AGENTS.md` greps the unanchored word 'git' — it substring-matches (e.g. 'review_git'); anchor it (\b, ^$, or a quoted key like '"git":')
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile AC #3 and AC #5: either add `CLAUDE.local.md` to the grep-check exclusion list in AC #5, or remove `CLAUDE.local.md` from the permitted-locations list in AC #3 (and clarify where automation-scoped bot identity lives).
evidence: AC #3: 'Any retained Joe-specific identity is explicitly scoped to the automation that owns it (e.g. `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `CLAUDE.local.md`)'
AC #5 grep exclusion list: 'excluding `infra/`, `.github/workflows/`, `tests/`, and `docs/experiments/`' — `CLAUDE.local.md` is absent
Cross-section contradiction: a file allowed by AC #3 would be flagged by the check mandated in AC #5
findings.finding: AC #3 names `CLAUDE.local.md` as a legitimate location to retain the bot identity, but AC #5's grep exclusion list omits `CLAUDE.local.md`, so the lint check would fire a false-positive failure on that file — contradicting AC #3's explicit allowance.
scenarios: An implementer follows AC #3, leaves bot identity in CLAUDE.local.md, adds the grep check from AC #5, runs `make lint`, and gets a failing build — two ACs cannot both be satisfied simultaneously.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `CLAUDE.local.md` to the explicit exclusion list in AC #5's grep command, e.g.: `git grep -nE '...' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md' ':(exclude)CLAUDE.local.md'` (or pass it via `--exclude`), and note `CLAUDE.local.md` alongside the other intentional exclusions.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `CLAUDE.local.md` to the Remediation's cascade flag (either as an in-scope file to update or as an explicitly justified out-of-scope exclusion) so it is not an orphan reference appearing only in AC #3.
evidence: Remediation cascade flag: 'verify all imported/copied agent guidance (`AGENTS.md`, `.agents/rules/*.md`, and any other contributor-facing guidance under `docs/`)' — no mention of CLAUDE.local.md
AC #3 introduces `CLAUDE.local.md` without any corresponding described work in the Remediation body
findings.finding: `CLAUDE.local.md` is referenced in AC #3 as a scoped location for bot identity, but the Remediation's cascade sweep description never mentions it — leaving implementers without guidance on whether it should be reviewed, modified, or left untouched.
findings.location: Remediation — cascade flag paragraph; Acceptance Criteria — AC #3
scenarios: An implementer reads the Remediation to understand the full scope of the sweep, does not discover that CLAUDE.local.md needs attention, then fails AC #3's qualitative check during review.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Remediation cascade flag, add a sentence such as: 'If `CLAUDE.local.md` exists and is contributor-facing, verify it does not prescribe the bot identity as general guidance; if it is automation-scoped, add it to the explicit exclusion list in the lint check.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Convert the consistency-check grep from a file-allowlist to an inverse-exclusion form (e.g. `git grep ... -- ':!infra/' ':!.github/workflows/' ':!tests/' ':!docs/experiments/'` with no file restriction) so that all contributor-facing files, including CLAUDE.local.md and root-level docs, are covered by default and only the explicitly out-of-scope automation paths are excluded.
evidence: Plan grep: `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`
AC3 names CLAUDE.local.md as a legitimate retention site but CLAUDE.local.md is neither in the grep scan list nor in the explicit exclusion list

Remediation says 'verify all imported/copied agent guidance … and any other contributor-facing guidance under docs/' but the check does not cover files outside docs/ (e.g. root-level CLAUDE.local.md, README.md)
findings.finding: The consistency-check grep command is scoped to a fixed allowlist of files (AGENTS.md, .agents/rules/*.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, docs/*.md) rather than scanning all contributor-facing guidance and excluding known automation paths. Files like CLAUDE.local.md, README.md, or any future onboarding doc added outside docs/ will never be scanned, so reintroduced hardcoded identity in those files would silently pass lint — contradicting the plan's stated goal of a deterministic consistency check.
findings.location: Remediation section — grep command; AC item 5
scenarios: A future contributor adds DCO guidance to CLAUDE.local.md with the hardcoded bot identity; make lint passes silently because the file is outside the scanned allowlist.
README.md or a new root-level onboarding file is created with the bot identity; the grep never sees it.
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace the allowlist-style pathspec with an exclusion-style pathspec:
```
git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' \
  -- ':!infra/' ':!.github/workflows/' ':!tests/' ':!docs/experiments/'
```
This scans everything except the intentionally excluded automation paths, so new contributor-facing files are automatically covered without requiring the grep command to be updated.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a sentence to the Remediation section stating the positive rationale for the `make lint`-integrated grep approach (e.g., that it aligns with the repo's existing gate pattern where zizmor, actionlint, and module-size checks all live in `make lint`, ensuring the check runs in CI's `Verified` gate without a separate CI step).
evidence: Remediation says 'Add a deterministic consistency check: a make lint-integrated grep step' without stating why this is the preferred vehicle.
No sentence in the plan explains why make lint (vs. pre-commit, vs. a standalone CI step) is the right integration point.
ABSENCE: no positive rationale for approach selection anywhere in the plan text.
findings.finding: The plan's Remediation section describes what mechanism to implement (grep in make lint) but provides no positive rationale for WHY this approach was chosen — i.e., why a `make lint`-integrated grep step is the right fit here rather than, say, a pre-commit config entry or a CI-only step. The absence of a stated positive rationale leaves the implementer without authoritative guidance if they encounter friction wiring the check.
findings.location: Remediation section (paragraph beginning 'Add a deterministic consistency check')
scenarios: Implementer is unsure whether to add a Makefile target or a scripts/ script, and picks the wrong integration depth.
A future reviewer questions why the check lives in make lint rather than as a pre-commit rule, and the plan provides no answer.
findings.suggested_fix: After the sentence 'Add a deterministic consistency check', insert: 'This approach is chosen because `make lint` is already the canonical single-source gate that CI's `Verified` step mirrors 1:1 (per the Makefile header comment), so the check runs on every CI push without needing a new workflow step or pre-commit-only coverage.'

findings.finding: AC 5 requires a deliverable — 'a documentation consistency test or equivalent deterministic check' — that has no corresponding described work anywhere in the plan body (Finding, Evidence, or Remediation). The Remediation describes only documentation edits and a manual cascade verification sweep; no test/check implementation is mentioned. This AC is an orphan criterion that an implementer has no guidance to satisfy.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC 5 vs. Remediation section
findings.suggested_fix: In the Remediation section, add a step such as: 'Add a CI lint check (e.g., a grep/awk rule in a Makefile target or a pre-commit hook) that fails if any file outside the scoped automation guidance file contains a hardcoded Joe Oakhart sign-off pattern. Document which file owns the check.'

findings.finding: AC5 mandates 'a documentation consistency test or equivalent deterministic check' to prevent reintroducing a globally hardcoded personal sign-off identity, but this is an entirely new artifact that does not exist anywhere in the repo and the plan body provides no specification for it — no file path, form (CI step, pytest fixture, shell script), or mechanism is described.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Remediation bullet such as: 'Add a CI lint step (e.g., in `.github/workflows/lint.yml` or as a `Makefile` target) that runs `git grep -n "Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart" AGENTS.md .agents/rules/rebar.md CONTRIBUTING.md docs/ examples/` and fails if any match is found outside bot-scoped automation paths (`infra/`, `.github/workflows/`).'

findings.finding: AC5 mandates 'a documentation consistency test or equivalent deterministic check' to prevent reintroducing a globally hardcoded personal sign-off identity, but the plan does not specify what form this artifact takes (e.g., CI grep, pre-commit hook, linter rule), where it lives in the repository, or how it is maintained — leaving a new enforcement contract with no implementation spec.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sub-bullet under AC5 such as: 'Implemented as a CI lint step (e.g., `.github/workflows/doc-lint.yml`) that greps for the literal string `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` outside any file path explicitly scoped to bot-automation workflows and fails if found.'

findings.finding: AC5's 'documentation consistency test or equivalent deterministic check' is mechanistically under-specified: the codebase legitimately contains `joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` in automation scripts (infra/scripts/reviewbot-ensure-tickets.sh), CI workflows (.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml, reconcile-bridge-canary.yml), test fixtures (tests/fixtures/review_bot_merge/), and experiment corpora (docs/experiments/). A grep-based CI check without a defined exclusion scope will either false-positive on these legitimate uses or require an undocumented allowlist, leaving the mechanism unimplementable as written.
findings.suggested_fix: Refine AC5 to read: 'A CI grep check (e.g., in `make lint` or a dedicated test) scans contributor-facing guidance files (`AGENTS.md`, `.agents/rules/*.md`, `docs/*.md`, `examples/`) for the literal string `joeoakhart+bot` and fails if found; automation-owned paths (`infra/`, `tests/`, `.github/workflows/`) and experiment corpora (`docs/experiments/`) are explicitly out of scope for this check.'

findings.finding: No AC has a concrete proving command — every criterion relies on prose description with no grep, diff, or script cited to produce evidence of satisfaction.
findings.suggested_fix:
Append a 'Verification' block with commands such as:
```
# AC1 — identity-neutral instruction present
grep -q 'git config user.name' AGENTS.md && grep -q 'git config user.email' AGENTS.md

# AC2 — bot identity absent from general guidance
! grep -rE 'joeoakhart\+bot' .agents/rules/rebar.md AGENTS.md

# AC5 — consistency check exists and is executable
bash scripts/check-dco-identity.sh  # or equivalent named script
```

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Update AC5's grep pattern (or the proposed lint script) to cover `docs/**/*.md` recursively (or explicitly enumerate subdirs like `docs/adr/*.md`), matching the Remediation's stated intent to cover all contributor-facing guidance under `docs/`.
evidence: AC5 grep command: `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`

docs/ subdirectories confirmed present: docs/adr/, docs/design/, docs/calibration/, docs/research/ — none currently contain bot identity, but the lint check would not catch a future reintroduction there
Remediation says 'verify all imported/copied agent guidance ... and any other contributor-facing guidance under docs/' — this broader intent is not matched by the non-recursive glob
findings.finding: The AC5 lint grep pattern `'docs/*.md'` only matches top-level files in docs/ — it does not recursively scan docs/adr/*.md, docs/design/*.md, or other subdirectories. The Remediation's cascade instruction says to verify 'any other contributor-facing guidance under docs/', but the proposed deterministic check does not enforce that for subdirectories, leaving a gap if bot identity were introduced in, e.g., docs/adr/ or docs/design/.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC5 / Remediation cascade flag
scenarios: A contributor adds a docs/adr/ decision record or docs/design/ doc that re-hardcodes the bot sign-off; the new lint gate passes silently because the glob only hits docs/*.md top-level.
findings.suggested_fix: Change the grep invocation to use `git grep -nE '...' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/**/*.md'` (git grep supports `**` globbing), or add an explicit second pattern `'docs/adr/*.md' 'docs/design/*.md'` alongside `'docs/*.md'`; document `docs/experiments/` as the excluded subtree.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Fix the `docs/*.md` glob in the lint check to be recursive (e.g. `docs/**/*.md` with `git grep`'s pathspec, or replace with `$(git ls-files 'docs/*.md' 'docs/**/*.md')`) and explicitly exclude `docs/experiments/` using git grep's `:(exclude)docs/experiments/` pathspec, so subdirectory contributor docs are actually covered.
evidence: Plan prescribes: `git grep -nE '...' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`
Shell glob `docs/*.md` matches only files at the top level of docs/, not docs/adr/*.md, docs/design/*.md, docs/migrations/*.md, docs/research/*.md
The repo's docs/ directory contains subdirs: adr/, design/, archive/, research/, migrations/, experiments/ — all contributor-accessible
Stated exclusion 'explicitly excluding docs/experiments/' is already implicit in the non-recursive glob, suggesting the intent was to scan all docs/ except experiments/, not just the top level
findings.finding: The prescribed grep glob `docs/*.md` is non-recursive and will silently miss all contributor-facing docs under `docs/` subdirectories (e.g. `docs/adr/`, `docs/design/`, `docs/migrations/`, `docs/research/`). The stated exclusion of `docs/experiments/` is redundant (the glob already can't reach it), but more importantly any reintroduction of the hardcoded identity in a subdirectory doc would not fail the build — defeating the deterministic regression guard.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria AC5 / Remediation (grep command specification)
scenarios: A future contributor adds a `Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart <joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com>` example to docs/adr/0050-something.md — the lint check passes, the regression is not caught.
The bot identity is reintroduced in docs/design/ or docs/migrations/ guidance docs and the guard does not fire.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace `'docs/*.md'` with `'docs/**/*.md' ':(exclude)docs/experiments/**'` in the git grep invocation, or use shell expansion: `$(git ls-files 'docs/' | grep '\.md$' | grep -v '^docs/experiments/')`. Document that the glob is intentionally recursive to cover all subdirectory guidance.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace `'docs/*.md'` in the AC5 grep pathspec with a recursive form (e.g., `'docs/**/*.md'` if run via bash with globstar, or enumerate the subdirs, or omit the path restriction and rely solely on the explicit exclusions) so the lint check catches reintroduction anywhere under `docs/`, not just at the top level.
evidence: Plan prescribes: `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`
Shell glob `docs/*.md` expands to only files directly under docs/, not docs/adr/, docs/design/, docs/migrations/, etc.
Repo has populated subdirs: docs/adr/, docs/design/, docs/migrations/ confirmed in list_directory output.
Evidence class: Verified (observed) — shell glob expansion semantics are deterministic; docs/ subdirectory existence confirmed from directory listing.
The plan's own cascade note says 'verify all imported/copied agent guidance under docs/' without restricting to top-level only.
findings.finding: The plan's AC5 grep command uses 'docs/*.md' as a pathspec, but this shell glob only matches top-level files in docs/ and does NOT recursively match contributor-facing guidance in subdirectories such as docs/adr/*.md, docs/design/*.md, or docs/migrations/*.md. A reintroduced hardcoded identity in those subdirectories would silently escape the lint check.
findings.location: AC5 / Remediation section — grep command: `git grep -nE '...' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' 'docs/*.md'`
scenarios: A future contributor adds guidance referencing the bot identity to docs/adr/0050-dco-policy.md or docs/design/contributor-workflow.md; the lint check passes because docs/*.md does not match that path, defeating the regression guard.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `git grep -nE 'joeoakhart\+bot@navapbc\.com|Signed-off-by: Joe Oakhart' -- AGENTS.md '.agents/rules/*.md' 'CONTRIBUTING.md' ':(glob)docs/**/*.md'` (git's own `:(glob)` pathspec magic, which is recursive and shell-independent), or in the Makefile shell target use `find docs -name '*.md' ! -path 'docs/experiments/*'` to build the file list.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
